market_summary: Mixed-to-risk-on regime for the 30-day horizon: SPY +4.91%, QQQ +6.30%, and IWM +8.42% over 1 month, with all three above MA50 and MA200; breadth is healthy and small caps are leading, while TLT is flat/soft.

Current book remains aligned with leadership: KLAC, AVGO, TSM, GOOGL, and NVDA all beat SPY/QQQ on 1m or 3m measures and remain above MA50/MA200; energy has cooled near term but still retains strong 3m/6m trends, and COST remains low-volatility ballast despite weak short-term tape.

No-go for rebalance today: cadence is not due, expected alpha from off-cycle turnover does not clearly clear trading-cost drag, and the required qualitative refresh did not verify severe thesis damage in the refreshed subset.

thesis_damage_flags.why: Pack shows short-term tape deterioration: 1m -5.23%, below MA50, and lagging SPY/QQQ despite still-strong 3m/6m trend; qualitative thesis-damage evidence from refresh is UNKNOWN.
Pack shows weak near-term momentum: 1m -1.70%, below MA50, and momentum composite only +2.83%; qualitative thesis-damage evidence from refresh is UNKNOWN.
No severe break, but 3m return of +0.41% trails QQQ +1.91% and drawdown is relatively deep at -20.37%; qualitative thesis-damage evidence from refresh is UNKNOWN.

thesis: Catalyst: broad equity breadth remains supportive and the current portfolio already owns the strongest pack leadership in semicap/AI infrastructure plus energy and defensive ballast.

Factor edge: portfolio momentum remains favorable overall, with KLAC +21.55% 1m, AVGO +22.35%, TSM +10.56%, GOOGL +10.33%, and NVDA +8.54%; off-cycle turnover edge is marginal versus trading-cost drag.
Variant view: XOM, CVX, and COST are the soft spots, but weekly spacing discipline should hold unless verified qualitative damage escalates beyond the current UNKNOWN follow-up.
risks: Macro: a rates or growth shock could hit the portfolio's technology concentration even in a currently risk-on tape.
Idiosyncratic: semicap leadership is crowded and high-volatility; energy could keep lagging if commodity and crude momentum continue to fade.
Execution/liquidity: unnecessary off-cycle turnover one day after the last successful run would add friction without a clearly superior 30-day setup.
falsifiable_checks: Reassess at the next scheduled rebalance if both XOM and CVX remain below MA50 and continue to underperform SPY over the 30-day window.
Reassess sooner if COST loses MA200 support or if any of KLAC/TSM/AVGO/NVDA breaks below MA50 with worsening 30d relative strength versus QQQ.
trade_of_the_day.why_now: Base: regime is supportive, breadth is improving, and the existing book still captures the best quality+momentum leadership, so no trade best clears cost drag. Bull: semicap and AI infrastructure leadership persists and energy stabilizes, allowing current winners to compound over the next 30 days. Bear: energy and COST continue to lag, but without verified severe thesis damage the expected gain from immediate turnover does not exceed implementation friction.

constraints_check.notes: Appendix: factor highlights KLAC/TSM/AVGO strongest momentum leaders; GOOGL/NVDA still constructive; XOM/CVX weak 1m but solid 3m/6m; COST low-vol ballast but weak short-term. Fundamentals/news refresh: UNKNOWN for headline details and key ratios on NVDA/GOOGL/XOM/COST, so confidence reduced and no override to spacing discipline. Risk budget: IT 50%, Energy 28%, Comm Services 14%, Consumer Staples 8%; max position 15%; no crypto. Scenario stress: equity growth shock hurts IT concentration most, oil-down shock hits XOM/CVX, rates-up mixed, USD shock manageable via domestic-heavy mix plus TSM ADR risk. Execution plan: no trade today; preserve turnover efficiency until scheduled weekly checkpoint. Monitoring/kill-switch: reconsider if XOM+CVX both stay below MA50 and underperform SPY through next review, if COST breaks MA200, or if any core semi leader loses MA50 with weakening relative strength.
